source: classic real-world business-logic bug (incomplete Gregorian leap-year rule)

expected_bug.summary: Only checks year % 4 == 0. The Gregorian rule also excludes century years not divisible by 400: 1900 and 2100 are NOT leap years but this returns True; 2000 is correct only by luck. Produces a wrong Feb-29 / day-count for those years. A logic-completeness bug, not a type or boundary error.
expected_bug.l_code_hint: L5 (control flow / incomplete predicate) or domain-logic

fixed_change: Apply the full rule: year % 4 == 0 and (year % 100 != 0 or year % 400 == 0).
